Home / Research Projects / phish-triage-mcp: Local-First Phishing Triage MCP Server

phish-triage-mcp: Local-First Phishing Triage MCP Server

Local-first MCP server that performs static analysis of suspicious emails, SMS, and URLs without contacting external services.

Defensive AIMCPPhishing TriagePrivacy
View code

Overview

A defensive MCP server with purely static analysis tools for emails, SMS, and URLs. Parses RFC 5322 headers, reads Authentication-Results for SPF/DKIM/DMARC, detects lookalike domains, anchor-vs-href tricks, urgency phrasing, and OTP / UPI / KYC scam patterns. URLs are defanged and PII redacted by default. Auto-prefers safe-evidence-redactor when present.

Methods

Static header parsing, lookalike-domain detection, PII redaction, MCP tooling.

Evaluation

Released open-source with unit-tested detection rules.

Key Contributions

  • Static-only analysis: never visits suspicious URLs or follows redirects.
  • SPF/DKIM/DMARC parsing with plain-language explanations.
  • PII redaction (emails, phones, OTPs, card numbers, UPI) and URL defanging.
  • Indian-context scam pattern library (UPI, Aadhaar, KYC, courier).
  • Plug-and-play with Claude Desktop, VS Code Copilot, and other MCP clients.

Research & Engineering Challenges

  • Detecting phishing patterns purely statically without remote reputation lookups.
  • Building a precise lookalike-domain scorer with low false-positive rates.
  • Designing PII redaction that preserves debuggability while never leaking secrets.

Tech Stack

TypeScript Node.js MCP SDK Vitest

Status

Released: v0.2.0

Author
